text.title: Microservices Circuit Breaker Examples
text.summary: Worked example: wrap payment gateway POST /v1/charges with Resilience4j.

example.context: Spring Boot service calling Stripe-equivalent gateway. p99 latency 1.5s; failure spikes during peak. Fallback: queue the charge for async retry + return 202 Accepted.
example.walk: Step 1: identify call = PaymentClient.charge(). Fallback = enqueue ChargeJob + return Accepted. Step 2: config — failureRate=50, slidingWindow=50, minCalls=10, slowCallDuration=3000ms, waitOpen=10000ms, halfOpenCalls=3. Step 3: decorator = Decorators.ofSupplier(() -> client.charge(req)).withCircuitBreaker(paymentBreaker).withRetry(retry3WithBackoff).withFallback(ex -> chargeQueue.enqueue(req)).decorate().get(). Step 4: Micrometer scrapes resilience4j.* metrics; alert on payment-gateway state=open > 60s. Step 5: toxiproxy injects 503 to gateway for 30s → breaker opens at call #11 → fallback enqueues 40 charges → downstream recovers → breaker half-opens at t+10s → 3 probe calls succeed → breaker closes → fallback queue drained.
example.output: p99 caller latency stays under SLO during downstream degradation; 0 customer-visible payment errors; queued charges drained automatically.
